bindings.quest_action: repair the old meeting bridge between the feuding settlements
bindings.quest_focus: the three-generation feud over a water dispute
bindings.quest_stakes: both sides are stockpiling weapons and headed toward war
bindings.quest_threat: hatred so old that nobody alive remembers how it started
bindings.quest_origin: a friendship across the river forbidden by both families

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, you're not from either settlement, which makes you the only person who might actually listen to both sides.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Two settlements have been locked in {quest_focus} for generations over water rights. My closest friend lives on the other side. {target_npc} carries messages between us in secret, but {quest_stakes}. I need {quest_item} to {quest_action}. If there's neutral ground for talking, maybe reason has a chance.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_threat}, and for what? A river with enough water for everyone. Help me build a bridge: the real kind and the kind that matters more.

playerResponses.text: A bridge for peace. I'll gather what you need to build it.
Three generations is a long time to hate. But bridges are a good start.
Getting between feuding settlements is a fast way to make enemies on both sides.

bindings.quest_action: complete a dangerous proving task to earn resident status
bindings.quest_focus: the elder council's refusal to grant an outsider belonging
bindings.quest_stakes: without resident status I can't own property, join the militia, or attend feasts
bindings.quest_threat: the council insists outsiders bring trouble despite all evidence otherwise
bindings.quest_origin: six months of hard work, learning customs, and contributing to every harvest

dialogueChunks.intro: I've been told I don't belong here, {player_name}. Not in those words, but in every way that counts.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I arrived in {location} six months ago and found a community worth staying for. {quest_origin}, but {quest_focus} stands firm. {target_npc} has been advocating for me, but {quest_threat}. There's an old tradition: {quest_action} earns the right to stay. The task is gathering {quest_item} from a dangerous location {location_hint} where the {enemy_type} dwell.
dialogueChunks.outro: I don't want charity. I want to earn my place. But {quest_stakes}, and nobody here will help an outsider. Except you.

playerResponses.text: You've already proven your worth. Let's make the council see it too.
Earning belonging through a dangerous quest. That's a hard tradition, but I'll help.
If they don't want you, maybe you should find a community that does.

bindings.quest_action: reunite a split heirloom from the ruin where both halves are kept
bindings.quest_focus: the divide between artisan families and farming families
bindings.quest_stakes: both of us will be disowned if our families discover the relationship
bindings.quest_threat: decades of separation have hardened into rules nobody questions
bindings.quest_origin: a shared heirloom split during the original community separation

dialogueChunks.intro: Have you ever cared about someone you're not supposed to care about, {player_name}? Not by your own choice, but by everyone else's rules?
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} in {location} runs deep. Different quarters, different festivals. The person I want to spend my life with is from the other side. {target_npc} has been helping us meet in secret, but {quest_stakes}. I want to {quest_action} from {location_hint}: {quest_origin} that might remind people we were one community once.
dialogueChunks.outro: A divided heirloom for a divided settlement. If I can make the pieces fit together again, maybe {quest_threat} will finally crack.

playerResponses.text: Reuniting an heirloom to reunite a community. That's beautiful. I'll help.
Old divisions and forbidden bonds. You're either brave or foolish. Either way, I'm in.
Community divisions this deep don't heal with symbols. I'm sorry.
